One advisory source covers every ecosystem or private package.

permission.required_user_approval_text:
What permission is requested:
Approve VNEM Tools MCP to perform: external_fetch

Exact action: external_fetch
Risk level: medium
Why it is needed: Allowed by profile with explicit per-call approval.
Scope: Only the exact command/API/action shown above.
Dry-run option: available and should be run first
Rollback/restore plan: Keep dry-run output and do not apply without a backup/restore path.
What could go wrong: files may be changed incorrectly, commands may fail, API calls may expose metadata, or evidence may be incomplete.
Logs/evidence collected: evidence JSON under C:\VNEM\vnem-src\.tmp\dependency-security-fixture-57924\.vnem\evidence with secrets redacted.
What happens if approved: Tools MCP may run only the exact approved action within allowlists and limits.
What happens if denied: Tools MCP will stop after planning/dry-run and make no real change.
risk_notes: Sensitive local information or secret-like path/value.
permission.safer_alternative: Run dry-run first, then execute only the exact approved scoped action.
must_not_claim: Tools MCP performed this action before evidence exists.
external_fetch was approved without explicit user approval text.

limitations: Fixture install uses an allowed local package and does not prove public-registry install availability.
Live npm advisory proof covers this public npm snapshot only; it does not prove vulnerability absence across all ecosystems or private packages.
